title: Talk on the Rock: Calleigh Miller on sourdough and her micro bakery – Clearwater Baking
summary: Calleigh Miller talks about her business, Clearwater Baking, at KSTK on April 29, 2025. (Colette Czarnecki/KSTK) Episode 54: Imagine visiting a tiny structure, filled with baked goods and instructions on how to pay through the honor system. That’s Wrangell’s Clearwater Baking. title: Lawmakers, Indigenous Leaders, and Conservationists Reintroduce Arctic Refuge Wilderness Bill Amid Unprecedented Threats

title: School district forms ad hoc committee for budget crisis
summary: Ryan Howe speaks to Wrangell School Board on April 14, 2025 about the budget ad hoc committee the district recently formed. (Colette Czarnecki/KSTK) About 30 people packed the school board meeting room at Evergreen Elementary on April 14. Ryan Howe was one of them. title: AST Probation Violation: Tetlin [AK25039699]
summary: On April 27, 2025 at about 3:33 AM, Alaska State Troopers responded to the report of a disturbance in Tetlin. Subsequent investigation over the next two days resulted in Lavelle Jimmie, age 48 of Tetlin, being arrested in Tok for Probation Violation. Lavelle was transported and remanded to Fairbanks...

title: Sen. Lisa Murkowski makes pitch for renewable energy’s value in Alaska communities
summary: Yereth Rosen Alaska Beacon In a political environment where the president and his administration are pushing for more fossil fuel development and scorning alternative energy, Alaska’s senior U.S. senator is defending renewable projects in the rural areas of her state. U.S. Sen. Lisa Murkowski, R-Alaska, speaking at an Anchorage conference on Tuesday, recounted her efforts to convince Interior Secretary Doug Burgum that wind, solar and other sources of renewable energy are valuable even in oil-producing Alaska. In a phone call Monday that lasted more than an hour and in which Bu...

title: AST Arrest Warrant x5: Wasilla [AK25037398]
summary: On 4/29/2025, at approximately 0900 hours, the Alaska State Troopers received an anonymous tip that Conor Carvalho, age 27 of Wasilla has multiple arrest warrants and is currently at a residence on Mulchatna in Wasilla. AST responded to the scene. AST observed Conor in the driveway and Conor quickly...

title: Break up on the Tanana: The Tripod has fallen
summary: Break up is underway on the Tanana River at Nenana. The tripod of the Nenana Ice Classic fell over on April 27 at 3:56 PM AK Standard Time. This was preceded by much anticipation in the previous few days as ice conditions visibly deteriorated. Since 1917, Alaskans have places betts on the exact date...

title: AST Violating Conditions of Release: North Pole [AK25040441]
summary: On April 29, 2025, at approximately 0702 hours, AST responded to a verbal disturbance report in the area of Long Circle in North Pole. Investigation revealed that Rachel Okpeaha was violating her conditions of release. Rachel was arrested and transported to FCC on VCOR charge.

title: AST Drug Trafficking: Fairbanks [AK25000868]
summary: On April 24, 2025, the Fairbanks Areawide Narcotics Team Statewide Drug Enforcement Unit (FANT) arrested four people involved in drug trafficking in the Fairbanks and North Pole areas. FANT began investigating the group in late January, including through surveillance, serving residential search warrants and conducting high-risk vehicle stops. During the investigation, officers seized over 6.6 lbs of cocaine, 4.4 lbs of methamphetamine and over $10,000 cash. The four people arrested on charges of misconduct involving a controlled substance include: 40-year-old Willie Wilson of Fairbanks, 28-year-old Jarkese Pridgeon of Florida, 73-year-old Alton Keller of Fairbanks, and 38-year-old Tremain Wilson of Texas. The investigation is ongoing. FANT is made up of law enforcement from the Alaska Stat
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: charges of misconduct involving a controlled substance include: 40-year-old Willie Wilson

title: Learn the basics of running for local office at free workshop on May 3
